reviews.author: Kuppuswamy Sathyanarayanan
reviews.summary:
reviewer pointed out that the patch does not address the issue of preserving the upper 32 bits of RAX during 32-bit port I/O operations, and suggested adding a comment to clarify this

reviewer requested that the commit log include a link to the bug report or related discussion to provide context for the issue being fixed
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Kirill,

On 3/31/2026 4:24 AM, Kiryl Shutsemau (Meta) wrote:

---

LGTM. Can you include a link to the bug report or related discussion in 
the commit log? It will help understand the impact of this issue.

Reviewed-by: Kuppuswamy Sathyanarayanan <sathyanarayanan.kuppuswamy@linux.intel.com>

reviews.author: Kuppuswamy Sathyanarayanan
reviews.summary:
reviewer pointed out that the patch does not address a related issue in tdx.c where the emulation preserves the upper 32 bits of RAX during 32-bit port I/O operations, and requested that this be fixed

Gave Reviewed-by

Reviewer suggested a simplified conditional statement to improve code readability, replacing the existing logic that sets regs->ax based on size and mask

reviews.author: Huang, Kai
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the patch description does not accurately reflect the relevant section of the SDM (System Developer's Manual) regarding zero-extension for 32-bit port I/O operations, and suggested referencing Chapter 3.4.1.1 General-Purpose Registers in 64-Bit Mode.

Gave Reviewed-by
reviews.raw_body:
FWIW, the relevant part in the SDM seems to be:

  Chapter 3.4.1.1 General-Purpose Registers in 64-Bit Mode

  ...
  * 32-bit operands generate a 32-bit result, zero-extended to a 64 bit 
    result in the destination general-purpose register.

2026-03-31.patch_summary: This patch series addresses two technical inaccuracies in the TDX guest port I/O emulation code, specifically an off-by-one error in GENMASK() macro usage and incorrect zero-extension for 32-bit port I/O operations. The patches fix these issues by correcting the mask calculation and ensuring that the result is correctly extended to a full 64-bit RAX register. These changes were introduced in the initial implementation of runtime hypercalls for port I/O and are necessary to maintain accurate emulation of x86 architecture requirements.

reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the patch series addresses two technical inaccuracies in TDX guest port I/O emulation code, confirming that both patches are relevant to the issues at hand.
reviews.raw_body:
Link: https://lore.kernel.org/all/CAKw_Dz96rfSQc6Rn+9QBcUFHhmkK+9zu+P=bxowfZwxrATCBRg@mail.gmail.com/

It is relevant for both.

-- 
  Kiryl Shutsemau / Kirill A. Shutemov

2026-04-01.patch_summary: This patch series addresses two technical inaccuracies in the TDX guest port I/O emulation code, specifically an off-by-one error in GENMASK() macro usage and incorrect zero-extension for 32-bit port I/O operations. The patches fix these issues by correcting the mask calculation and ensuring that the result is correctly extended to a full 64-bit RAX register. These changes were introduced in the initial implementation of runtime hypercalls for port I/O and are necessary to maintain accurate emulation of x86 architecture requirements.
